behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is between manifest parsing, transitive-resolution logic (often via lockfile/resolver module), and the handoff to the vulnerability-check input set. A unit test of one parser can miss dropped transitive deps during module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scanner on fixture projects (e.g., npm and pip) with known direct+transitive trees, using real manifest/lock files on disk and a strict local fake vulnerability client that records requested packages/versions. Assert the queried set exactly matches the expected full dependency closure.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on the real external API contract and request semantics (version-range fields, encoding, filtering behavior). Mocks often accept overly broad requests and can hide real mismatches where only package name is honored.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability DB test endpoint (or recorded real contract environment), submit dependencies with same package names but different vulnerable/non-vulnerable ranges. Assert returned CVEs match only the manifest’s exact ranges and that out-of-range CVEs are excluded.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic within one function/object boundary. No I/O or cross-module behavior is required to expose failures.
behaviors.test_description: Provide an unsorted in-memory CVE list covering all severities and tied severities with different CVSS values; assert exact output order: severity rank first, then descending CVSS within severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at parser/editor/serializer interaction and file round-trip behavior (preserving unrelated entries). A unit test of only version-selection logic can miss unintended rewrites.
behaviors.test_description: Use real manifest files with multiple dependencies and formatting quirks. Run the update flow for one vulnerable package, then diff before/after and assert exactly one dependency version changed and all other keys/values remain identical.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: This crosses real external boundaries (Git hosting API, branch/PR creation, actual repo diff). Mocked PR clients can hide auth, branch, and diff-generation failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against a disposable real repository, trigger one safe patch bump, and assert a real PR exists with exactly one dependency line changed in the diff and expected metadata (title/body/branch).

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: This is semver comparison logic and is deterministic without I/O. Failures are local to parsing/comparison rules.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for version pairs (including pre-release/build metadata cases) asserting breaking=true only for major bumps per semver policy.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is orchestration: semver decision output must gate PR module invocation and propagate a review flag to scanner output. This is cross-module control flow, not external-API correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner pipeline with input that requires a major bump, wiring real decision/output modules and a spy/fake PR client. Assert PR client is never called and scanner output includes the breaking-change review flag with affected dependency details.
